question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $90,000 on December 2?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price Analysis 2025: Trends & Expert Forecasts
evidences.content: The market sentiment for Bitcoin in late 2025 appears cautiously optimistic, with technical indicators suggesting a potential for growth, though short-term fluctuations are to be expected. The influence of institutional investment and broader economic conditions will likely be key drivers. [...] ### Long-Term Growth Trajectory Despite the short-term choppiness, the long-term outlook for Bitcoin often points towards a sustained growth trajectory. Historical data, while not a guarantee of future results, suggests a pattern of higher highs and higher lows over extended periods. The introduction of financial products like Spot Bitcoin ETFs has also been cited as a factor potentially contributing to increased demand and, consequently, long-term value appreciation. Analysts often look at the overall market capitalization, which currently stands at approximately $2.21 trillion, as an indicator of Bitcoin’s established presence and potential for continued expansion within the global financial landscape. ## Technical Indicators and Market Sentiment [...] ### Projected Minimum and Maximum Values Forecasting exact price points is challenging, but many analyses within the Bitcoin price anal...

evidences.content: Message Format ``` {"e": "continuous_kline",// Event type "E": 1607443058651,// Event time "ps": "BTCUSDT",// Pair "ct": "PERPETUAL"// Contract type "k":{"t": 1607443020000,// Kline start time "T": 1607443079999,// Kline close time "i": "1m",// Interval "f": 116467658886,// First trade ID "L": 116468012423,// Last trade ID "o":"18787.00",// Open price "c":"18804.04",// Close price "h":"18804.04",// High price "l":"18786.54",// Low price "v":"197.664",// volume "n": 543,// Number of trades "x": false,// Is this kline closed ? "q":"3715253.19494",// Quote asset volume "V":"184.769",// Taker buy volume "Q":"3472925.84746",// Taker buy quote asset volume "B": "0"// Ignore}}< pair> _< contractType> @continuousKline_< interval> ``` [...] Supported kline intervals: : seconds: 1s minutes: 1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m hours: 1h, 2h, 4h, 6h, 8h, 12h days: 1d, 3d weeks: 1w months: 1M Notes: : If startTime/endTime not specified, returns most recent klines Supported timeZone values: : + Hours and minutes (e.g. “-1:00”, “05:45”) + Only hours (e.g. “0”, “8”, “4”) + Accepted range is strictly [-12:00 to +14:00] inclusive If timeZone provided, kline intervals are interpreted in that timezone instead of...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Market Outlook and Key Levels
evidences.content: Forward outlook: market at a critical juncture with mixed signals. Key level: $90k is an important resistance area for breakout confirmation, while $85k is a critical support floor. Watch for ETF flow reversal, stablecoin minting resuming as a capital-return signal, and volatility expansion as a possible regime-change indicator.

evidences.title: Bitcoin ETF Flows as a Market Driver
evidences.content: Bitcoin ETF flows can be an important market driver, but everyday ETF metrics are not a definitive indicator of future pricing. To evaluate whether the correction is entering its final cyclical stage, monitor whether major ETFs post consecutive daily inflows to confirm institutional dip-buying rather than short-lived tactical bounces.

evidences.title: [PDF] Federal Register/Vol. 88, No. 137/Wednesday, July 19, 2023/Notices
evidences.content: markets have grown and evolved as well. The market for bitcoin is approximately 100 times larger, having at one point reached a market cap of over $1 trillion.24 According to the CME Bitcoin Futures Report, from February 13, 2023 through March 27, 2023, CFTC regulated bitcoin futures represented between $750 million and $3.2 billion in notional trading volume on Chicago Mercantile Exchange (‘‘CME’’) (‘‘Bitcoin Futures’’) on a daily basis and notional volume was never below $670 million.25 Open interest was over $1.4 billion for the entirety of the period and at one point was over $2 billion. ETPs that primarily hold CME Bitcoin Futures have raised over $1 billion dollars in assets. The CFTC has exercised its regulatory jurisdiction in bringing a number of enforcement actions related to [...] a Bitcoin Futures ETF is potentially more susceptible to potential manipulation than a Spot Bitcoin ETP that offers only in-kind creation and redemption because settlement of CME Bitcoin Futures pricing (and thus the value of the underlying holdings of a Bitcoin Futures ETF) occurs at a single price derived from spot bitcoin pricing, while shares of a Spot Bitcoin ETP would represent interes...

evidences.title: API Client to Binance
evidences.content: binancer 1.2.0 Reference # binancer An R client to the Public Rest API for Binance. API docs: Quick intro to using `binancer` by getting the most recent ~USD price of the cryptocurrencies supported on Binance: ``` library(binancer) library(binancer)binance_coins_prices() binance_coins_prices() ``` Getting data on a specific symbol pair, e.g. the most recent Bitcoin/USDT changes: ``` (klines <- binance_klines('BTCUSDT', interval = '1m'))(klines<- binance_klines('BTCUSDT' = '1m')) ``` Visualize this data, e.g. on a simple line chart: ``` library(ggplot2) library(ggplot2)ggplot(klines, aes(close_time, close)) + geom_line() ggplot(klines aes(close_time close)) + geom_line() ``` Poor man’s candle chart with some `ggplot2` tweaks: [...] library(data.table) library(data.table)klines <- rbindlist(lapply(klines<- rbindlist(lapply( c('ETHBTC', 'ARKBTC', 'NEOBTC', 'IOTABTC'), c('ETHBTC' 'ARKBTC' 'NEOBTC' 'IOTABTC') binance_klines, binance_klines interval = '15m', = '15m' limit = 424)) = 4 24))ggplot(klines, aes(open_time)) + ggplot(klines aes(open_time)) + geom_linerange(aes(ymin = open, ymax = close, color = close < open), size = 2) + geom_linerange(aes(= open = close = close< open) = 2)...

evidences.title: Stuck in Limbo - Glassnode Insights
evidences.content: ## Conclusion Bitcoin continues to trade within a mild bearish range, defined by strong resistance between $106K–$118K and key structural support near $97.5K–$100K. The market remains under the influence of top-heavy supply, with short-term holders still realizing losses. On-chain data highlighted a brief accumulation near $100K, suggesting localized support, yet without a decisive reclaim of the short-term holders’ cost basis (~$111.9), upside momentum is likely to stay constrained. Off-chain indicators also echo this cautious tone. ETF outflows, muted funding rates, and low open interest point to subdued speculative engagement, while options traders continue to favor downside protection around the $100K line. [...] Bitcoin remains in a mild bearish phase, trading between $97K and $111.9K, with resistance near $116K marked by top-buyers’ supply cluster. Seller exhaustion and renewed accumulation near $100K provide short-term support but lack strong follow-through demand. A dense supply cluster between $106K–$118K continues to cap rallies as investors exit near breakeven. ETF flows have turned modestly negative, reflecting fading institutional demand and a cautious risk appetite...

evidences.content: Skip to content Deribit Insights Logo Categories + Industry + Option Flows + Exchange Updates + Market Research + Education + Podcast + Out of Office Options Course Trading Tools Partners Dev Hub KB English # Crypto Derivatives: Analytics Report – Week 45 Block Scholes2025-11-05T15:55:47+00:00November 5, 2025|Industry| Weekly recap of the crypto derivatives markets by BlockScholes. Key Insights: [...] While US risk-on equities have held steady over the past week, crypto spot prices have continued to fall lower, souring sentiment further in derivatives markets. Since Oct 10, 2025, BTC has primarily traded between $105K and $115K. Earlier today, it broke below the $105K support level – that coincided with a further drop in our Risk- Appetite Index, designed to capture market sentiment. Funding rates on perpetual swap contracts have fallen to their lowest levels (-0.005% on an eight-hourly basis) since early August 2025, while volatility smiles have skewed further towards protection against more downside price action. Meanwhile, short-tenor options for both BTC and ETH have outperformed, suggesting traders are pricing in greater volatility expectations in the near-term. For ETH, th...

The GitHub repository can be found here. [...] ## Using the code This code is free for all to use and edit as they wish. I hope each program is useful as it is, but they are also meant to be building blocks with which you can build your own more personalised applications. For example, even if you’re not too interested in the max pain calculation itself, displaying the current open interest by strike and option type may well be something useful you want to incorporate into your own code. evidences.title: Navigating BTC's Resistance: The Bear Call Spread Play - Deribit Insights
evidences.content: Deribit Insights Logo # Navigating BTC’s Resistance: The Bear Call Spread Play ## View on market Capitalizing on high Open Interest and approaching resistance, traders can use the bear call spread strategy for the current market. ## Bear Call Spread The proposed strategy is a Bear Call Spread. A bear call spread is achieved by simultaneously selling a call option and buying a call option at a higher strike price but with the same expiration date. You might consider initiating this trade if you believe that BTC can face resistance from the overhead Resistance zone. ## Trade Structure (OTM Call) Sell 1x BTC-26APR24-$70,000-C @ $730 (OTM Call) Buy 1x BTC-26APR24-$71,000-C @ $545 Target: Spot level < $70,000 ## Payouts Maximum Profit: $185/BTC ## Why are we taking this trade? [...] Maximum Profit: $185/BTC ## Why are we taking this trade? BTC experienced some pullbacks after reaching a low of around $59,650, bouncing back from the 4-hour demand zone following several tests of this zone. Currently, there’s a strong overhead supply zone or resistance near $66,600, which could pose a significant challenge for BTC’s upward movement. Moreover, there’s a high Open Interest (OI) at the $70...
